vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely non-representative of the current visual data. P1 (String of Nickels) is not visible. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is represented only by the white rabbit anchor; the plant itself is absent or entirely obscured.

inventory_reconciliation.p1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected in yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.p2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage.
inventory_reconciliation.p3: Systemic Loss: Pothos foliage absent; only anchor remains.
inventory_reconciliation.p4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage.

plant_audit.p2_p4_black_pot: Contains soil and eggshell debris; no viable plant growth.
plant_audit.p3_black_pot: Contains white rabbit anchor; no Pothos foliage visible.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistently dark, appears damp/saturated across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Significant eggshell fragments present in the foreground black pot.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: A single, small, pale green sprout (likely a weed or uncatalogued seedling) is visible in the foreground pot, emerging near the eggshells.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_current: The sequence shows a transition from a state of near-total botanical absence to the emergence of a single, uncatalogued sprout in the foreground pot. No registered plants have shown growth or presence throughout the 5-day window.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently in a state of failure regarding the registered species. The presence of a single, unidentified sprout suggests the soil remains viable for opportunistic growth, but the registered plants (P1-P4) are effectively lost or dormant to the point of invisibility.
anomalies: Uncatalogued green sprout in foreground pot.
Presence of eggshell fragments as soil amendment/top dressing.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a systemic loss of all registered botanical specimens. The desk environment is currently hosting a 'ghost' biome where the pots are present but the intended plants are not. The only biological activity is a singular, unidentified sprout in the foreground pot, which does not match the morphology of the Pothos, Mexican Mint, or String of Nickels. The soil appears consistently damp, suggesting potential over-watering or lack of transpiration due to the absence of foliage.
